title: oxytropis glabra var. salina
intro: Oxytropis glabra var. salina, also known as Salt-leaved Locoweed, is a variant of the Oxytropis glabra plant, a perennial legume native to the salt lakes of the western United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a natural diuretic and to reduce inflammation. The plant is said to offer various health benefits, such as aiding in the treatment of skin conditions, reducing fever, and providing relief from respiratory issues. Traditionally, the plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or dried extracts, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
